name: Optical Flow Particles
description: CPU optical-flow surrogate (previous-frame difference + blur, non-CUDA) extracted from a video source, driving both a flow-warped noise texture (flow_out) and a particle render. The motion energy is measured (topto → analyze average → flow_level) and a CHOP Execute callback scales the particleSOP's turbulence and wind with it, so motion in the source visibly stirs the particles out of the box. Swap 'source' for a videodeviceinTOP to drive it from a live camera (the default movie file is a static image, so expect a calm field until a moving source is wired in). Live-validated in TouchDesigner 099 build 2025.32820.

nodes.name: source
nodes.comment: Video source (swap to videodeviceinTOP for live camera). Default file is a static sample image — wire a moving source for flow.

nodes.name: pre_blur
nodes.comment: Spatial pre-blur to suppress high-frequency noise

nodes.name: mono
nodes.comment: Luminance for differencing

nodes.name: cache_prev
nodes.comment: Previous frame for temporal diff (outputindex -1 = one frame back)

nodes.name: flow
nodes.comment: Absolute frame difference = motion energy

nodes.name: flow_blur
nodes.comment: Smooth the motion energy into a soft displacement field

nodes.name: flow_gain
nodes.comment: Amplifies the (small) frame-difference so displace and the particle drive respond; downsampled so the CHOP readback stays cheap

nodes.name: noise_field
nodes.comment: Base noise that gets warped by optical flow

nodes.name: displace
nodes.comment: Warps noise by motion energy; midpoint 0 so no motion = no displacement

nodes.name: flow_out
nodes.comment: Flow-warped noise texture (also useful as a projection layer)

nodes.name: flow_to_chop
nodes.comment: Reads the (64x64) motion-energy texture into CHOP samples

nodes.name: flow_analyze
nodes.comment: Overall motion energy as a single value

nodes.name: flow_level
nodes.comment: Stable motion-energy level (0 = still, rises with movement)

nodes.name: flow_drive
nodes.comment: Pushes motion energy into the particle sim (turbulence + wind) via its callback

nodes.name: geo
nodes.comment: Renders the particle SOP nested inside it

nodes.name: emitter
nodes.comment: Grid emitter — particles spawn from grid vertices

nodes.name: particle
nodes.comment: Particle simulation rendered as point sprites (default 'lines' is invisible); baseline turbulence keeps it alive, flow_drive raises it with motion

nodes.name: sprite_tex
nodes.comment: Soft radial dot used as the sprite texture, so particles read as glow points

nodes.name: mat
nodes.comment: Glowing sprite material (pointsize > 1 so particles are actually visible)

nodes.name: render
nodes.comment: Final particle render

nodes.name: bg_dim
nodes.comment: Darkened flow texture as the moody backdrop (brightness, not opacity, so alpha stays 1)

nodes.name: comp
nodes.comment: Particles composited over the dark flow backdrop

python_code.flow_drive:
def onValueChange(channel, sampleIndex, val, prev):
    p = op('geo/particle').par
    k = max(0.0, min(val * 6.0, 2.5))
    p.turbx = 0.3 + k
    p.turby = 0.3 + k
    p.turbz = 0.3 + k
    p.windx = k * 0.4
    return

preview_description: A dark field of glowing particle sprites over a flow-warped noise texture (flow_out). Motion in the video source raises the measured flow level, which stirs the particles with turbulence and wind — stand still and it calms, move and it churns. Live-validated in TouchDesigner 099 build 2025.32820.
